translation.text: You cannot be president unless you are a natural born citizen.
We won't go on the trip unless it stops raining.
You can't make an omelet unless you break a few eggs.
The car will not start unless you turn the key.
Unless valid evidence is presented, the theory cannot be accepted.
The package will not be delivered unless someone is home to sign for it.
Unless you water the garden, the plants will die.
Discounts are not applicable unless you are a member.
Unless the council approves the budget, the project halts.
He will not resign unless coerced.
Access is denied unless you have security clearance.
Unless we leave now, we will miss the flight.
The transformation is impossible unless the catalyst is present.
You cannot pass the bar exam unless you study law.
Unless the market recovers, layoffs are inevitable.
Peace cannot be achieved unless both sides compromise.
The software won't compile unless all errors are fixed.
Unless precautions are taken, the virus will spread.
No refund is given unless proof of purchase is shown.
Unless the dam is repaired, the village will flood.
You will succeed only if you work hard.
The plant grows only if it receives sunlight.
I will attend the party only if you come with me.
You can enter the restricted area only if you have a badge.
A number is even only if it is divisible by two.
The contract is valid only if signed by both parties.
Birds fly only if they have wings.
Use the emergency exit only if there is a fire.
The light turns green only if the sensor detects a car.
You get dessert only if you eat your vegetables.
He performs well only if he feels confident.
The engine runs only if there is fuel.
We win the game only if we score now.
The law applies only if the crime was committed in this state.
She sings only if the audience is quiet.
Oxygen is liquid only if it is extremely cold.
The meeting happens only if the CEO is available.
You are eligible only if you are over 18.
The software updates only if connected to WiFi.
Apples fall only if gravity exists.
If it rains, the ground gets wet.
If you study, you will pass the test.
If the alarm rings, we must evacuate.
If the temperature drops, water freezes.
If you break the law, you will be punished.
If X is a square, then X is a rectangle.
If Socrates is a man, then Socrates is mortal.
If the key fits, the door opens.
If you mix blue and yellow, you get green.
If there is smoke, there is fire.
If the demand increases, the price rises.
If the batter hits a home run, the crowd cheers.
If you are a doctor, you have a medical degree.
If the verdict is guilty, the defendant appeals.
If the sun sets, it gets dark.
If the premises are true, the conclusion is true.
If you miss the bus, you will be late.
If the foundation is weak, the building collapses.
If you sign up early, you get a discount.
If the voltage is too high, the fuse blows.
All cats are mammals.
Every student must take the exam.
All roads lead to Rome.
All politicians are public servants.
Every rose has its thorn.
All valid arguments have true conclusions if their premises are true.
Every citizen has the right to vote.
All lawyers pass the bar exam.
Every living thing needs water.
All prime numbers greater than 2 are odd.
Anyone who enters must pay.
All that glitters is not gold.
Everyone loves a winner.
All electrons have a negative charge.
Every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
All triangles have three sides.
Any breach of contract results in a lawsuit.
All birds lay eggs.
Every cloud has a silver lining.
All integers are rational numbers.
No dogs are cats.
No one who lies is trustworthy.
No squares are circles.
No man is an island.
No planet is a star.
No reptiles have fur.
No odd number is divisible by 2.
No minors are allowed.
No good deed goes unpunished.
translation.q: Unpunished (Careful: This effectively means P -> ~Q where Q is 'goes unpunished' or P -> Q if Q is 'punished')

translation.text: No herbivores eat meat.
No one lives forever.
No smoking is permitted.
No two snowflakes are alike.
No king rules alone.
No penguins can fly.
No justice, no peace.
No risk, no reward.
No vacancy available.
No parking on Sundays.
No questions asked.

symbolic.text:
Find the contrapositive of:
P → E

Find the contrapositive of:
I → Y

Find the contrapositive of:
H → L

Find the contrapositive of:
Q → S

Find the contrapositive of:
B → G

Premises:
1) F → Q
2) Q → G
Infer?

Premises:
1) X → H
2) H → N
Infer?

Premises:
1) M → T
2) T → E
Infer?

Premises:
1) G → P
2) P → L
Infer?

Premises:
1) E → H
2) H → P
Infer?

Premises:
1) B → K
2) K → F
Infer?

Premises:
1) S → Y
2) Y → O
Infer?

Premises:
1) B → N
2) N → W
Infer?
Translate: It is impossible that R
Translate: It is impossible that T
Translate: It is impossible that U

Translate: It is impossible that L

Translate: It is impossible that M
Translate: It is impossible that R
Translate: It is impossible that U

negation.text: Negate: All federal judges are appointed for life.
negation.answer: Some federal judges are NOT appointed for life.

negation.text: Negate: All prime numbers are odd.
negation.answer: Some prime numbers are NOT odd.

negation.text: Negate: All birds are capable of flight.
negation.answer: Some birds are NOT capable of flight.

negation.text: Negate: No defendants are guilty before trial.
negation.answer: Some defendants ARE guilty before trial.

negation.text: Negate: No minors are allowed to vote.
negation.answer: Some minors ARE allowed to vote.
Half or fewer voters are decided.
Half or fewer students are prepared.
Half or fewer businesses are small.
Half or fewer cities are polluted.
Half or fewer diets are healthy.
Half or fewer investments are safe.
Half or fewer applications are bug-free.
Half or fewer arguments are valid.
Half or fewer politicians are ambitious.
Half or fewer athletes are fit.
Half or fewer movies are entertaining.
Half or fewer critics are harsh.
Half or fewer solutions are complex.
Half or fewer trends are temporary.
Half or fewer workers are satisfied.
Half or fewer clients are happy.
Half or fewer prices are stable.
Half or fewer rules are strict.
Half or fewer winters are cold.
Half or fewer friends are loyal.

negation.text: Negate: If it rains, then the ground gets wet.
negation.answer: Even if it rains, it is NOT necessarily true that the ground gets wet (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If you study, then you pass.
negation.answer: Even if you study, it is NOT necessarily true that you pass (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If you eat, then you are full.
negation.answer: Even if you eat, it is NOT necessarily true that you are full (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If it snows, then school is closed.
negation.answer: Even if it snows, it is NOT necessarily true that school is closed (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If the alarm rings, then we leave.
negation.answer: Even if the alarm rings, it is NOT necessarily true that we leave (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If price drops, then demand increases.
negation.answer: Even if price drops, it is NOT necessarily true that demand increases (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If crime occurs, then police investigate.
negation.answer: Even if crime occurs, it is NOT necessarily true that police investigate (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If contract is signed, then deal is closed.
negation.answer: Even if contract is signed, it is NOT necessarily true that deal is closed (Counterexample).

negation.answer: Even if you apologize, it is NOT necessarily true that I forgive you (Counterexample).

negation.answer: Even if sun sets, it is NOT necessarily true that it gets dark (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If battery dies, then phone turns off.

negation.text: Negate: If law passes, then taxes rise.
negation.answer: Even if law passes, it is NOT necessarily true that taxes rise (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If stock crashes, then investors panic.
negation.answer: Even if stock crashes, it is NOT necessarily true that investors panic (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If joke is funny, then people laugh.
negation.answer: Even if joke is funny, it is NOT necessarily true that people laugh (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If door is locked, then key is needed.
negation.answer: Even if door is locked, it is NOT necessarily true that key is needed (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If virus spreads, then quarantine begins.
negation.answer: Even if virus spreads, it is NOT necessarily true that quarantine begins (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If water boils, then steam rises.
negation.answer: Even if water boils, it is NOT necessarily true that steam rises (Counterexample).

negation.text: Negate: If mirror breaks, then bad luck follows.
negation.answer: Even if mirror breaks, it is NOT necessarily true that bad luck follows (Counterexample).
